hero_intro: Management Information Systems student and Web Developer.
bio: I am a 4th-year student of Management Information Systems and a software developer who loves end-to-end project development. I implement web and mobile solutions that businesses need in their digitalization processes, supporting them with robust database architectures. My biggest motivation is to analyze customer requirements correctly and build high-performance systems that manage themselves without the need for a technical team. I prefer not to just stay behind the code, but to take an active role in the entire journey of a project, from the analysis phase to deployment on the server and going live.
ozbag_desc: Involved in web development using Laravel and React with PostgreSQL. Managed project flow using Git and Trello, and participated in team meetings.
keles_desc: Modernized the municipality website using ASP.NET Core MVC. Conducted analysis meetings with the Mayor and managers.
ironsan_desc: Worked on Luca accounting application integration and the improvement of the existing ERP system.
freelance_desc: Web projects along with domain, hosting, and Cloudflare processes for businesses in various sectors.
q1: 1. Which technologies do you work with?
a1: I actively work with Laravel, React, ASP.NET Core, Flutter, PostgreSQL, and MySQL.
q2: 2. How do you handle a project?
a2: Analysis → Design → Development → Testing → Live deployment.
q3: 3. What is your freelance project duration?
a3: Depending on the size of the project, it usually takes 1 to 4 weeks.
q4: 4. Do you have experience working in a team?
a4: Yes, I have worked with teams at Özbağ Foundation, Keles Municipality, and Ironsan Metal.
q5: 5. What is included in the delivery?
a5: Source code, technical documentation, and live URL.
q6: 6. How do you manage your projects as a student?
a6: I use Git, Agile methodologies, and project management tools effectively.
subject: Subject
service1_desc: I design and code high-performance, SEO-friendly, and modern web projects that truly reflect your brand's digital identity.
service2_desc: I build custom, secure backend systems that allow you to easily manage all your content dynamically without needing technical knowledge.
service3_desc: Beyond just coding, I handle the complete deployment process, including domain configuration, hosting setup, SSL, and Cloudflare integration.
service4_desc: With expertise in both Front-end and Back-end technologies, I code end-to-end solutions, building independent and powerful systems from database to UI.
service5_desc: I develop high-performance, modernly designed mobile applications that run seamlessly on both iOS and Android from a single codebase using Flutter.
service6_desc: From the initial idea and requirement analysis to the deployment phase, I provide strategic consulting to help you choose the best architectural technologies.
car_rental_desc: A comprehensive web project featuring reservation and vehicle management processes, developed using ASP.NET Core MVC and N-Tier architecture. I built this as a university course project, using MSSQL as the database. The project includes core modules such as user registration and login, vehicle listing and filtering, online reservation creation, and vehicle & customer management through an admin panel. I adopted a layered architecture (Data Access, Business Logic, Presentation) to ensure a sustainable and scalable infrastructure.
smoke_quit_desc: A mobile application I developed using Flutter that visualizes users' progress and provides motivation.
ozbag_project_desc: In the dynamic web project I developed for the Özbağ Foundation using Laravel, React, and PostgreSQL, I built an infrastructure where staff can manage content without needing a technical team. While managing professional workflows with Git, I experienced the software development processes end-to-end through technical documentation I prepared and presentations I made to the team.
keles_project_desc: I handled the design process of this corporate web project for Keles Municipality from start to finish. The back-end infrastructure was built using the ASP.NET Core MVC framework, with MySQL as the database. Throughout the development phase, I held regular requirements analysis and technical design evaluation meetings with the Keles Mayor and the Head of R&D. This process provided me with invaluable corporate experience in translating the real-world needs of a public institution into technical solutions.
casanuez_desc: In the corporate website project I developed as a freelance developer for CasaNuez, I built the system using the Laravel framework and PostgreSQL database infrastructure. One of the key features of the project is the integration of custom Telegram bots that manage real-time reservation notifications. I personally managed the entire deployment lifecycle end-to-end, including domain purchasing, hosting setup, Cloudflare security configuration, and DNS routing, delivering a fully operational, turn-key solution to my client.
koza_bazaar_desc: In the e-commerce hackathon competition where I participated with my 5-member team 'Ahi Koza' and secured 3rd place, we were randomly assigned nuts and herbalist products to market. I developed the 'Koza Bazaar' project by analyzing how we could export these products to the most suitable global markets in the most optimal and profitable way. Working closely with my team, I led the business analysis, market research, and export feasibility processes, presenting our project's core value proposition to the jury.
